plan.failureSummary: Click on age radio option failed: unable to resolve target element using any of the candidate selectors. Page remained on first survey step instead of advancing toward Thank You page.
observedIndicators: Donobu click tool failed with message 'Unable to resolve HTML element to operate with.'
All candidate selectors for age option ([data-testid='radio-age-25-34'], structural nth-of-type radio selectors) resolved to no element.
Failure occurred during deterministic PageAi run with cached instructions, but the failure is at selector resolution time, not at assertion time.
Historical pass rate is high (0.84) with recent deterministic successes, indicating a recent UI or attribute change rather than chronic flakiness.
Failure screenshot shows survey still on step 1 with name and email populated but no age selection; Thank You page was not reached.
Baseline screenshot from last success shows Thank You page, confirming end-to-end flow normally works.

remediationSteps.summary: Update Donobu/PageAi selectors for the age radio option.
remediationSteps.details: Inspect the current DOM for the age/selection step on https://unstable-survey-dinoer.replit.app. Verify whether [data-testid='radio-age-25-34'] or the fallback structural selectors still exist. If attributes or structure changed, update the SmartSelector configuration in the cached script (tests/.cache-lock/unstable-survey.test.ts.cache.js or underlying Donobu tool configuration) to use a stable locator such as a new data-testid, accessible name, or role-based selector.

remediationSteps.summary: Regenerate or refresh the deterministic PageAi flow for this test.
remediationSteps.details: Because the run mode is DETERMINISTIC and the failing selector is embedded in the cached PageAi script, regenerate the PageAi instructions for 'Fill out all of the survey questions...' so it re-learns the updated UI. Delete the existing cache entry for unstable-survey.test.ts and let a fresh autonomous run record new interactions that correctly pick an age option.

remediationSteps.summary: Re-run the test after selector and cache updates.
remediationSteps.details: After fixing selectors or regenerating the deterministic flow, re-execute unstable-survey.test.ts in the same environment and confirm that the PageAi click on the age option succeeds and the test reaches the /thank-you URL.

remediationSteps.summary: Lightly increase waits around the selection step if intermittent.
remediationSteps.details: If, after selector fixes, occasional failures persist, add an explicit wait for the age radio group to be visible/enabled before clicking. For example, wait for locator('role=radio[name="25-34"]').first().toBeVisible() before invoking the click tool, to guard against slow renders.

remediationSteps.summary: Manually compare failing DOM to last successful run if uncertainty remains.
remediationSteps.details: If selector updates do not resolve the issue, open Playwright traces or DevTools for a failing and a passing run. Manually confirm whether any conditional rendering, A/B tests, or feature flags are affecting the age selection UI, and adjust automation accordingly.

additionalDataRequests.description: Current DOM and attributes for the age selection radio controls on the survey page at the time of failure.
suggestedSources: Live inspection via browser DevTools against the test environment

additionalDataRequests.description: Contents of tests/.cache-lock/unstable-survey.test.ts.cache.js around the failing click step to see the exact SmartSelector configuration.
suggestedSources: Repository cache-lock file in CI artifact or source control

additionalDataRequests.description: Any recent frontend changes to the survey form, especially around age/selection step or data-testid attributes.
suggestedSources: Git commit history for the survey form component

plan.notes: The failure is isolated to resolving a specific radio-button selector during a deterministic PageAi run. Other interactions (goToWebpage, inputFaker for name/email) succeed, and the application itself works when reached (as shown by baseline Thank You page). High historical pass rate plus selector lookup failure strongly indicate a locator/UI change rather than an application defect or generic flakiness. Clearing the PageAi cache and updating selectors should restore stability.
